Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office and the General Service Office to observe the perpetual inventory method following the procedures under Manual on NGAS for LGUs to fairly present the Inventories in conformity to PPSAS No. 12:
The local accountant should draw a Journal voucher (JV) to record the Inventories in the Journal and in the inventory ledger cards. These Supplies Ledger Cards are subsidiary ledgers maintained by the accounting office for each type of supplies, crops and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance and disposal.
The required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports should be prepared, maintained and submitted for the recognition of the expense portion of supplies inventories.
The General Service Office should maintain the Stock Card to be used to record all receipts and issuances of supplies. Issuances should be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), which the General Service Office shall summarize monthly in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). The RSMI together with the supporting RIS should be submitted to the local accountant within five days after the end of the month, and the RSMI should be the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuances. The Journal Voucher drawn based on the RSMI recognized the expense and the issuance of the Inventories.
At the end of the year, a Physical Count should be conducted to determine the undistributed/unconsumed items and the cost of these items should be restored back to the inventory account for any necessary adjustment in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation. The Report on the Physical Count of Inventories should be used to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date. It shows the balance of inventory items per cards and per count and shortage/overage, if any.
